pages.title: Overview
pages.purpose: Introduce the NPM-Simcompanies repository, explaining its purpose as a SimCompanies game automation tool and providing a high-level summary of its capabilities

pages.title: Repository Structure
pages.purpose: Describe the three files in the repository (simco.py, README.md, LICENSE) and explain their relative importance and roles

pages.title: Key Features and Capabilities
pages.purpose: List and briefly explain the main features: authentication, glove trading automation, multi-realm support, and anti-detection

pages.title: Getting Started
pages.purpose: Provide practical guidance for users who want to run the automation script, including prerequisites and execution steps

pages.title: Prerequisites and Dependencies
pages.purpose: Document required software (Python, Chrome, undetected_chromedriver, Selenium) and system requirements (Windows for registry access)

pages.title: User Input Guide
pages.purpose: Explain each user prompt (email, password, realm selection, quantities, prices) and how they affect the automation

pages.title: Running the Script
pages.purpose: Provide step-by-step instructions for executing simco.py and what to expect during execution

pages.title: System Architecture
pages.purpose: Present the high-level architectural design of the automation system, including its layers and main components

pages.title: External Dependencies
pages.purpose: Describe how the system interacts with external systems (SimCompanies website, Chrome browser, Windows Registry) and libraries

pages.title: The Simco Class
pages.purpose: Document the core Simco class, its responsibilities, and provide an overview of its six methods

pages.title: Initialization (__init__)
pages.purpose: Explain browser setup, ChromeOptions configuration, and anti-detection measures applied during initialization

pages.title: Start Method
pages.purpose: Document the start() method which navigates to SimCompanies and handles initial page setup including cookie consent

pages.title: Authentication Method
pages.purpose: Describe the authentication() method's login flow, including locating form fields and credential submission

pages.title: Buy Method
pages.purpose: Explain the buy() method's process for purchasing gloves, including realm switching and exchange navigation

pages.title: Sell Method
pages.purpose: Document the sell() method which handles listing gloves in individual stores with specified quantities and prices

pages.title: Extra Method
pages.purpose: Describe the extra() method and why it exists separately to handle two specific stores (store14 and store16)

pages.title: Anti-Detection Mechanisms
pages.purpose: Detail the techniques used to avoid bot detection: undetected_chromedriver, ChromeOptions flags, and navigator.webdriver modification

pages.title: Realm System
pages.purpose: Explain the concept of realms (R1 and R2) in SimCompanies and how the script handles realm-specific logic

pages.title: Realm Configuration
pages.purpose: Document how users select realms and how realm choice affects configuration (different variables: gni vs gni2, sli vs sli2)

pages.title: Realm-Specific Calculations
pages.purpose: Explain the purchase amount calculations that differ by realm (×13 for R1, ×14 for R2) and the business logic behind these multipliers

pages.title: Realm Switching Logic
pages.purpose: Document how the script handles realm switching in the buy() method when R2 is selected

pages.title: Store Management
pages.purpose: Explain how the script manages multiple in-game stores for selling gloves, including the store list and selling loop

pages.title: Store XPath List
pages.purpose: Document the hardcoded list of 13 store XPaths and explain the XPath pattern used to identify stores

pages.title: Main Selling Loop
pages.purpose: Describe how the script iterates through the store_xpath list and calls sell() for each store

pages.title: Extra Stores Architecture
pages.purpose: Explain the architectural decision to handle two additional stores (14 and 16) separately through the extra() method rather than the main loop

pages.title: Operational Workflows
pages.purpose: Present the complete end-to-end execution flow from script start to completion, showing how all components work together

pages.title: Main Execution Flow
pages.purpose: Document the sequential execution in the main block: instantiation, start, authentication, buy, sell loop, extra

pages.title: Data Flow
pages.purpose: Trace how user input data flows through calculations and transformations to eventually affect SimCompanies website state

pages.title: Timing and Synchronization
pages.purpose: Explain the wait strategies used, including WebDriverWait timeouts and fixed sleep durations throughout the workflow

pages.title: Technical Implementation Details
pages.purpose: Provide deep technical documentation of implementation choices, patterns, and low-level details
